design_philosophy.core_loop: Evade pursuing burglars while collecting family valuables and transporting them to safety via basement chute; navigate hazards in basement to reach safe room
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure and evasion: must avoid Wet Bandits while completing objective tasks across mansion wings
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Kevin McCallister defending his home through cunning and navigation rather than combat confrontation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Post-movie narrative continuation (villains return for revenge with backup) transforms familiar home setting into sprawling mansion with fantastical wings (toys, electronics, pets, haunted basement); prioritizes evasion/collection over trap-setting combat that defined the film

difficulty.lives_system: Lives system present; labeled as 'Lives' in US/EU version, 'Kevin' in Japanese version
difficulty.continues_system: Japanese version provides 3 continues; US/European versions provide only 1 continue
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Regional versions offer asymmetric difficulty tuning through continue availability (Japanese more forgiving with 3 continues vs Western 1 continue)

progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential mansion wing access implied by level structure (toys, electronics, pets, basement areas); basement progression gated behind completing collection objective
replayability_hooks: Hidden developer message easter eggs embedded in rooms (jump mechanics trigger character name pop-ups with point rewards)
Regional difficulty variation encourages replays with different continue allocations
Point-scoring system from developer messages suggests score-chasing potential

world_building_techniques: Mansion transformation from familiar McCallister home into elaborate multi-wing structure signals escalated threat
Thematic wing organization (toys, electronics, pets, haunted basement) creates world coherence despite fantastical expansion
Wet Bandits' return as vengeful antagonists with reinforcements establishes narrative stakes beyond film events

notable_bosses: Basement boss encounter (unspecified type; reached after navigating rats, bats, spiders, ghosts)

faction_indicators: Harry and Marv 'Wet Bandits' (antagonists/burglars with backup reinforcements)
danger_signals: Wet Bandits' return from jail with vengeful intent
Introduction of backup/reinforcements suggesting escalated threat level
Basement hazards (rats, bats, spiders, ghosts) signal increased environmental danger
environmental_storytelling: Mansion wing theming (toys, electronics, pets) implies valuables requiring protection
Basement setting with supernatural elements (ghosts) contrasts with upper-floor domesticity
Safe room destination establishes family wealth and security concerns

sources_used: tcrf - Home Alone (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
